title: Railway Deployment Setup
content:
# Railway Deployment Setup

## Project
- **Name:** checker
- **URL:** https://railway.com/project/196033fc-3b58-485e-ba6c-dd9ba86c51c9

## Environments

### Production
- **Domain:** https://checker-app-production.up.railway.app
- **Services:** checker-app (GitHub: imcitius/checker), Postgres
- **DATABASE_URL:** auto-provided via Postgres reference variable

### Staging
- **Domain:** https://checker-app-staging.up.railway.app
- **Services:** checker-app, Postgres (pending first deploy)
- **DATABASE_URL:** auto-provided via Postgres reference variable (resolves after Postgres deploys)

## Services

### checker-app
- Linked to GitHub repo: imcitius/checker
- Port: 8080
- Uses Dockerfile for builds
- Env vars: DATABASE_URL (ref to Postgres), PORT=8080

### Postgres
- Railway-managed PostgreSQL
- Private domain: postgres.railway.internal:5432
- Database: railway, User: postgres

## Env Vars Still Needed (manual)
These must be set manually per environment when Slack App is configured:
- SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
- SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET
- SLACK_DEFAULT_CHANNEL

```bash
railway variable set --service checker-app --environment production SLACK_BOT_TOKEN=xoxb-...
railway variable set --service checker-app --environment production SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET=...
railway variable set --service checker-app --environment production SLACK_DEFAULT_CHANNEL=C...
```

## Deploy Tokens (for CI/CD)
Generate per environment in Railway dashboard → Project Settings → Tokens.
Add to GitHub repo as secrets:
- RAILWAY_TOKEN_PRODUCTION
- RAILWAY_TOKEN_STAGING
